steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined phrase is 'much like the filters in 3D glasses once worn at movies.' The phrase uses an explicit comparison ('much like') to connect the mantis experiment's color filters to something familiar. The question asks what function this comparison serves in the text as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'identifies a potential problem researchers faced': the phrase doesn't describe any obstacle or difficulty — it simply draws a parallel to 3D glasses. ✗
  • Choice B — 'offers a comparison meant to aid understanding': 'much like 3D glasses' translates an unfamiliar experimental setup (color filters over insect eyes) into something readers already know, making the study easier to visualize. ✓
  • Choice C — 'emphasizes a difference between mantis research and other research': the phrase draws a similarity, not a difference, and it references movie glasses — not other animal research. ✗
  • Choice D — 'describes an earlier use of a tool the researchers used': 'once worn at movies' refers to how audiences used 3D glasses, not to any earlier use of a tool by the mantis researchers themselves. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
